items.briefing:
Australian schools, TAFEs, and registered training organisations are being urged to review their relationships with third-party software and service providers, after new reporting highlights how vendor breaches are causing serious disruption across the education sector. When a supplier is compromised, your organisation's data can be exposed even if your own systems are secure.

Separately, the Australian Communications and Media Authority (ACMA) has outlined its 2026–27 compliance priorities, with a strong focus on consumer protection — relevant to any RTO handling student data or communications. Scam activity also remains elevated. **Practical tip:** Ask your key software vendors how they notify you if they suffer a data breach — and make sure you have a contact on file to follow up quickly.

featured.title: Local Police Collusion Hampers Crackdown on Asian Scam Centers
featured.quote: Local police corruption enables Asian scam centers, directly threatening Australian citizens and economy.
